description:
A distributed-build environment stores its produced binaries in a flat
    directory, then keeps a very small plaintext “index” that downstream
    deployment tools read.  
    1.  The binaries themselves are in
        /home/user/artifact_storage/releases/  
    2.  The index that is supposed to list every binary (one file name per
        line, no extra whitespace) is in
        /home/user/artifact_manager/index/repository.index  
    3.  An empty directory already exists for any debug artefacts that are
        created during maintenance:
        /home/user/artifact_manager/debug/

    Right now the index is stale – at least one file is present in
    artifact_storage/releases/ but missing from the repository.index file.

    Your task as the “curator” is:

    •   Detect every binary that is present in
        /home/user/artifact_storage/releases/
        BUT absent from
        /home/user/artifact_manager/index/repository.index.

    •   Produce a log file named
        /home/user/artifact_manager/debug/missing_artifacts.log
        with the following exact, testable format:

            •   One **file name** per line.  
            •   Lines must appear in plain ASCII sort order
                (i.e. the order produced by “sort” with no extra options).  
            •   No leading or trailing whitespace on any line, and the file
                must end with a single trailing newline character.  

        Example of the required formatting rules (do **not** copy literally —
        real content will differ):

            alpha-1.0.0.bin
            bravo-3.5.2.bin
            charlie-2.1.4.bin
            <newline-at-EOF>

    •   Leave all other files unchanged; only create the single log file
        described above.

    A correct solution can be achieved with as little as two or three standard
    command-line invocations (think along the lines of “ls”, “sort”, “comm”,
    “diff”, redirection, etc.).  Use any utilities available in a typical
    POSIX-compliant shell, but **do not** assume root privileges.

    After the commands are issued:
    1.  /home/user/artifact_manager/debug/missing_artifacts.log must exist
        and be world-readable (default umask is fine).
    2.  Its contents must list every, and only, binary file name that is in
        releases/ but not yet recorded in repository.index, following the
        exact formatting rules above.

    No other output or side-effects are required.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem layout and contents before the agent starts:

    Directory: /home/user/artifact_storage/releases/
        • appserver-2.4.1.bin         (empty, 0-byte placeholder)
        • dbdriver-1.3.0.bin          (empty, 0-byte placeholder)
        • utils-0.9.8.bin             (empty, 0-byte placeholder)

    Directory: /home/user/artifact_manager/index/
        • repository.index  
          └── Contents (with trailing newline):
                appserver-2.4.1.bin
                dbdriver-1.3.0.bin

    Directory: /home/user/artifact_manager/debug/
        • (empty)

    Files/dirs have normal 0644 / 0755 permissions created under user:group
    “user:user”.

    Expected artefacts after the agent finishes:

    A new regular file must exist:
        /home/user/artifact_manager/debug/missing_artifacts.log

    Contents of that file must be exactly (including final newline):

        utils-0.9.8.bin\n
name: task_000000_8f259b8b
